Answer: b. 21

Reason (brief):
- Loan 1: Drawdown 2015-01-19. Regular payment dates are the 19th from 2015-02-19 to 2021-01-19 (72 dates).
- A date is “not a Business Day” if it falls on a weekend or a holiday listed (3 Apr 2015; 6 Apr 2015; 25 Oct 2015; 25 Mar 2016; 28 Mar 2016; 24 Oct 2016; 14 Apr 2017; 17 Apr 2017; 30 Oct 2017; 30 Mar 2018; 2 Apr 2018; 19 Oct 2018; 19 Apr 2019; 22 Apr 2019; 14 Oct 2019; 10 Apr 2020; 13 Apr 2020; 19 Oct 2020).

Count by year (regular dates on 19th):
- 2015: 2 (Apr 19 Sun; Dec 19 Sat)
- 2016: 3 (Mar 19 Sat; Jun 19 Sun; Nov 19 Sun)
- 2017: 4 (Feb 19 Sun; Mar 19 Sun; Aug 19 Sat; Nov 19 Sun)
- 2018: 2 (May 19 Sat; Aug 19 Sun)
- 2019: 4 (Jan 19 Sat; Apr 19 Sat [holiday]; May 19 Sun; Oct 19 Sat)
- 2020: 6 (Jan 19 Sun; Apr 19 Sun; Jul 19 Sun; Sep 19 Sat; Oct 19 a holiday; Dec 19 Sat)
- 2021: 0 (Jan 19 Tue is a business day)

Total = 2 + 3 + 4 + 2 + 4 + 6 + 0 = 21.

Thus, 21 of the 72 Regular Payment Dates are not Business Days.

Answer: a) 13%

Reason:
- Loan 2 terms: PV = 100,000, N = 48, annual rate = 7%, so monthly rate ≈ 0.07/12 ≈ 0.0058333.
- The monthly payment from the solution is about 2394.98.
- Total payments ≈ 2394.98 × 48 ≈ 114,959.
- Total interest ≈ 114,959 − 100,000 ≈ 14,959.
- Proportion of payments that are interest ≈ 14,959 / 114,959 ≈ 0.130, i.e., about 13%.

This aligns with the workbook’s computed result for Question 8 (0.130126, i.e., ~13%).
